problem: hlx tickets bundle produces 0 artifact files for PREVIEW_READY (and other non-active status) tickets because its artifact summary fetch omits the runId query parameter, while the working artifacts/artifact commands include it.

files.why: Primary command implementation. Line 43 fetches /tickets/:id/artifacts without runId. Line 36 resolves runId from ticket detail but only uses it at line 55 for individual step-artifact fetches. The artifacts summary call (line 43) returns empty stepArtifactSummary for non-active tickets, so the loop at line 53 iterates zero times.

Working comparison command. Lines 20-23 accept --run flag via getFlag and conditionally pass runId as queryParams to the same /tickets/:id/artifacts endpoint. This is why 'hlx tickets artifacts --run <id>' works for PREVIEW_READY tickets.

Working comparison command. Lines 29-39 accept --run flag, and when absent, resolve runId from ticket.currentRun?.id ?? ticket.runs[0]?.id (same fallback logic as bundle). Uses runId in the step-artifact fetch URL at line 44.
Subcommand router. Line 27 shows bundle usage has no --run flag. Lines 134-143 dispatch to cmdTicketsBundle. Lines 112-119 and 123-131 show artifacts/artifact commands accept --run.
Flag parsing utilities. getFlag (line 5) and requireFlag (line 28) are used by bundle and sibling commands. No --run flag is parsed in bundle.ts.
HTTP transport layer. hxFetch (line 37) accepts queryParams option at line 40. Bundle already uses queryParams for repoKey (line 58 of bundle.ts) but not for the artifact summary call.
Config/auth boundary. HxConfig type definition (line 13). Not directly related to bug but shared dependency.
Ticket ref resolution used by all ticket commands. Not directly impacted.
Shows richer TicketDetail type (lines 6-22) including status field. TicketDetail in bundle.ts (lines 7-11) is a minimal subset without status.
CLI entry point. Dispatches to runTickets at line 96.
Build/test/quality gates. Scripts: build=tsc, typecheck=tsc --noEmit, test=tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js. Version 1.3.2.
TypeScript config. strict mode enabled, target ES2022, module Node16.
CI pipeline: npm ci, npm test, version validation, tarball validation, npm publish.
Existing test file for flags module. No bundle-specific tests exist.
Existing test file for ticket resolution. No bundle-specific tests exist.

facts: bundle.ts line 43: fetches GET /api/tickets/:id/artifacts with no queryParams — this is the artifact summary call that returns empty stepArtifactSummary for non-active statuses.
bundle.ts line 36: resolves runId as ticket.currentRun?.id ?? ticket.runs[0]?.id — the runId IS available before the artifacts call but is not passed to it.
bundle.ts line 55: uses the resolved runId for individual step-artifact fetches /tickets/:id/runs/:runId/step-artifacts/:stepId, but this code is never reached when stepArtifactSummary is empty.
artifacts.ts lines 20-23: passes optional runId as queryParams to the same /api/tickets/:id/artifacts endpoint, making it work for non-active tickets.
artifact.ts lines 29-39: accepts --run flag and falls back to currentRun/runs[0] resolution — same pattern as bundle but also supports explicit run override.
tickets/index.ts line 27: bundle usage string shows no --run flag: 'hlx tickets bundle <ticket-ref> --out <dir>'.
tickets/index.ts line 25: artifacts usage shows --run flag: 'hlx tickets artifacts <ticket-ref> [--run <runId>]'.
tickets/index.ts line 26: artifact usage shows --run flag: 'hlx tickets artifact <ticket-ref> --step <stepId> --repo <repoKey> [--run <runId>]'.
bundle.ts TicketDetail type (lines 7-11) does not include status field; get.ts TicketDetail (lines 6-22) includes status.
No test files exist for bundle.ts. Only flags.test.ts and resolve-ticket.test.ts exist in src/lib/.
Quality gates: tsc (build), tsc --noEmit (typecheck), node --test (test runner). All in package.json scripts.
No ORM, migrations, or database dependencies — pure CLI tool with zero runtime dependencies.
unknowns: Server-side behavior: the exact API contract for GET /api/tickets/:id/artifacts — specifically whether passing runId as a query param fully resolves the empty response for non-active statuses, or whether there are additional status-specific behaviors.
Whether currentRun is null/undefined for PREVIEW_READY tickets or whether it is populated but the artifacts endpoint ignores it without an explicit runId parameter.
Whether ticket.runs[0] is the latest or earliest run — the ordering convention of the runs array is not documented in the CLI codebase.
Whether other non-active statuses beyond PREVIEW_READY, DEPLOYED, UNVERIFIED, FAILED also exhibit this behavior.
